steps.title: Identify what the sentence needs
steps.content: The sentence describes Arizona's lawmakers (multiple people) designating the state's official fossil. We need the plural form lawmakers and the singular possessive state's.

steps.title: Apply plural and possessive rules
steps.content:
Choice B correctly uses lawmakers (plural noun, no apostrophe) and state's (singular possessive).

Choices A and D use states instead of the possessive form, and choices C and D use lawmaker's (singular possessive when a plural noun is needed).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
